channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Erdogan gifts NATO leaders engraved revolvers with live ammunition
item.description: NATO’s leaders left the Ankara summit with guns and live ammunition gifted to them by Turkiye’s President.

item.title: At least 28 people killed as fire engulfs shoe factory in China
item.description: Beijing demands 'all-out' effort to extinguish the blaze, search for survivors and treat the injured.

item.title: ‘Not Nigel Farage’: Who is Count Binface, contesting as MP for Clacton, UK?
item.description: The joke candidate with a bin-shaped helmet is so far Farage's only challenger in the English constituency of Clacton.

item.title: ‘She doesn’t give up that easily’: France’s Le Pen eyes the Elysee
item.description: Marine Le Pen, convicted of embezzling EU funds, announces her plans to run in 2027 after winning an appeal.

item.title: Photos: Iran to bury slain supreme leader in culmination of mass funeral
item.description: Iranians gather in sweltering heat for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei’s funeral in his hometown of Mashhad.

item.title: FIFA World Cup: Which quarterfinal team has best chance to win the trophy?
item.description: Form guide on the final eight teams as they battle it out for the FIFA World Cup 2026 trophy in the United States.

item.title: Graham Platner drops Maine Senate bid: What that means for Democrats
item.description: Democrats are scrambling to field a candidate as popular as Platner to clinch Maine in the midterms.

item.title: EU lawmakers call for probe into FIFA’s Infantino
item.description: EU lawmakers call for probe into FIFA’s Infantino

item.title: Indonesian farmer goes viral using a drone as transportation
item.description: Indonesian farmer goes viral using a drone as transportation

item.title: Trump targets Spain, NATO backs Ukraine: Is the alliance still united?
item.description: While NATO members have agreed to boost defence spending, Trump's attacks on Spain and Greenland have threatened unity.

item.title: Mosque model placed on top of Northern Ireland bonfire
item.description: Pro-UK loyalists in Northern Ireland have placed a replica mosque at the top of a bonfire, which is due to be lit.

item.title: Ukraine targets Russian oil depots and tankers in drone attacks
item.description: Ukraine hit two oil tankers in Sea of Azov and several oil facilities across Russia in drone attacks.

item.title: Videos show interception of Iranian missiles in skies over Jordan
item.description: Sirens blared in several cities in Jordan as Iranian missiles were intercepted.

item.title: FIFA World Cup 2026: What happened to the beautiful game?
item.description: How the World Cup has changed over the past 50 years and why there are only flashes of the beautiful game in 2026.

item.title: Mediator Pakistan says renewed US-Iran conflict is ‘in no one’s interest’
item.description: Islamabad urges both sides to honour their MoU as renewed attacks raise fears of a wider regional conflict.

item.title: UK Labour leadership nominations begin: Who’s running and how it works
item.description: Former Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham is the frontrunner. If no rival emerges, he could become PM this month.

item.title: India floods sweep away thousands of gas cylinders
item.description: India floods sweep away thousands of gas cylinders

item.title: Deadly US strikes trigger Iranian attacks on Gulf states
item.description: The US military says it has struck 90 targets in Iran. Iran says it sent drone strikes on US-linked sites in the Gulf.

item.title: Watch World Cup Preview: Morocco plays France in quarterfinal opener
item.description: Latest match highlights, reaction and previews from the FIFA World Cup 2026 in North America.

item.title: Iran prepares to bury slain leader Khamenei after mass funeral processions
item.description: The late supreme leader will be buried in his hometown, the eastern holy city of Mashhad.

item.title: US strikes Iran for second night – is the peace process all over now?
item.description: Neither side has officially called off the interim peace agreement, despite the escalation.

item.title: UN probe finds mass killings, gang rapes by Sudan’s RSF amount to genocide
item.description: UN Fact-Finding Mission found that the paramilitary's systematic campaign of violence in Darfur amounted to genocide.

item.title: Australia to begin selling uranium to India for peaceful purposes
item.description: Australia to begin selling uranium to India for peaceful purposes

item.title: Air raid sirens in Bahrain after uneasy night of US-Iran strikes
item.description: After a tense night of overnight US-led strikes on Iran, air raid alerts sounded across Gulf states.

item.title: Who is Zeynep Sonmez, Wimbledon star with a ‘Palestine’ watermelon racket?
item.description: Turkish tennis player Zeynep Sonmez defies a Wimbledon ban to stand in solidarity with Palestine.
